experts.purpose: Evaluate parenting capacity, child attachment, home environment
experts.qualifications: Licensed psychologist, AFCC-certified, MI family law experience
what_they_assess: Each parent's willingness to foster relationship with other parent
Child's adjustment to home, school, community
Mental and physical health of all parties
History of domestic violence or substance abuse
experts.ifp_coverage: Court may appoint at county expense under MCL 552.505(1)(e)

experts.purpose: Document psychological impact of parental alienation on child
experts.qualifications: Licensed child psychologist, attachment theory expertise
what_they_assess: Child's emotional state and attachment patterns
Signs of parental alienation or coaching
Impact of separation from father on child wellbeing
experts.ifp_coverage: May be covered under child's insurance or court order

experts.purpose: Expert testimony on parental alienation dynamics
experts.qualifications: PhD/PsyD with forensic specialization, alienation research
what_they_assess: Child's resistance to relationship with targeted parent
experts.ifp_coverage: Court appointment possible — argue constitutional right to present defense

experts.purpose: Authenticate text messages, emails, social media evidence
experts.qualifications: Certified digital forensic examiner (EnCase, FTK certified)
what_they_assess: Text message extraction and chain of custody
experts.ifp_coverage: Unlikely covered — but critical for evidence authentication
experts.how_to_retain: Michigan Association of Digital Forensics professionals

experts.relevance: Lane A/E/F — Due process, §1983, judicial misconduct
experts.purpose: Expert testimony on constitutional violations in family court
experts.qualifications: Law professor, constitutional law specialty, published on family law due process
what_they_assess: 14th Amendment due process violations in custody proceedings
First Amendment right to petition (access to courts)
Equal protection violations (pro se vs. represented litigant)
Patterns of systematic due process denial
experts.estimated_cost: $300-500/hour consultation, may testify pro bono for significant case
experts.ifp_coverage: Some may consult pro bono for impactful constitutional cases
experts.how_to_retain: WMU Cooley, U of M, MSU Law — constitutional law faculty

experts.purpose: Limited-scope representation or consulting on pro se filings
experts.qualifications: Michigan licensed, family law specialist, pro se assistance experience
what_they_assess: Discovery strategy for pro se litigant
experts.ifp_coverage: Legal aid organizations may provide free limited consultation
experts.how_to_retain: Michigan State Bar Lawyer Referral Service, Legal Aid of Western Michigan

experts.purpose: Represent L.D.W.'s independent interests in custody proceedings
experts.qualifications: Michigan licensed attorney, GAL training per MCR 3.917(A)
what_they_assess: Both homes, both parents, relevant third parties
Recommendations to court on custody/parenting time
experts.estimated_cost: Court-appointed — county pays or split between parties
experts.ifp_coverage: YES — court appoints and covers cost
experts.how_to_retain: Motion to Appoint Guardian ad Litem (MCL 722.24)

experts.purpose: Document housing code violations at defendant's residence
experts.qualifications: Certified housing inspector, familiar with MI building codes
experts.ifp_coverage: City/county inspection may be free — file complaint with code enforcement
experts.how_to_retain: Contact Norton Shores Building Dept or hire private inspector

experts.relevance: Lane E — JTC Complaint against McNeill
experts.purpose: Expert analysis of judicial conduct violations
experts.qualifications: Retired judge, judicial ethics professor, or former JTC staff
what_they_assess: Canon violations (Michigan Code of Judicial Conduct)
Pattern of bias in rulings and orders
Due process deprivation by judicial officer
experts.estimated_cost: $200-500/hour, may consult on significant misconduct cases
experts.ifp_coverage: JTC complaint is FREE — no expert technically required
experts.how_to_retain: Michigan Judicial Institute contacts, retired judges

experts.purpose: Assess patterns of coercive control in co-parenting dynamic
experts.qualifications: Licensed social worker or psychologist with DV specialization
what_they_assess: Weaponization of court system against targeted parent
Impact of false allegations on accused parent
Berry's role in facilitating control patterns
experts.ifp_coverage: DV organizations may provide free assessments
experts.how_to_retain: Every Woman's Place (Muskegon), MI Coalition Against DV

free_resources.name: Legal Aid of Western Michigan
free_resources.service: Free family law consultation for qualifying income

free_resources.name: WMU Cooley Law School Clinic
free_resources.service: Free legal assistance from supervised law students

free_resources.name: Michigan Legal Help
free_resources.service: Free self-help resources and document assembly

free_resources.name: Muskegon County FOC
free_resources.service: Free mediation and parenting time enforcement

retention_strategy.phase_1: Consult family law attorney for filing review ($200-400)
retention_strategy.phase_2: Motion for GAL appointment (FREE — court appoints)
retention_strategy.phase_3: Motion for custody evaluation (may be court-funded under IFP)
retention_strategy.phase_4: Constitutional law professor consultation (may be pro bono for significant case)
retention_strategy.priority_order: GAL → Custody Evaluator → Family Law Consultant → Constitutional Expert
